label: Azure Cloud Platform
definition: The global Microsoft Azure cloud platform that hosts all AI, ML, data, and application services.
long_description: Microsoft Azure is a hyperscale cloud platform spanning many regions worldwide. It provides identity (Microsoft Entra ID), networking, compliance, billing, and global SLAs that underpin all downstream AI, ML, data, and application assets used in AI-300 and AI-103 solutions.

label: Microsoft Entra ID
definition: Azure's cloud identity and access management service used to secure users, apps, and workloads.
long_description: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) provides authentication, authorization, conditional access, and service principals for Azure resources. In AI-300 and AI-103, it secures access to Azure Machine Learning workspaces, Azure AI Foundry projects, Azure OpenAI deployments, Key Vault, and DevOps pipelines via managed identities and app registrations.

label: Azure Region
definition: A geographic location where Azure resources such as AI, ML, and data services are deployed.
long_description: An Azure region is a set of datacenters deployed within a latency-defined perimeter. AI-300 and AI-103 workloads must choose regions that support Azure Machine Learning, Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Search, and other AI services, while meeting data residency and compliance requirements.

label: App Registration
definition: An application identity in Microsoft Entra ID used to authenticate services and clients.
long_description: App registrations define application identities, redirect URIs, permissions, and secrets or certificates. In AI-300 and AI-103, they are often used for web apps, APIs, and tools that call Azure Machine Learning, Azure OpenAI, or Azure AI Foundry endpoints securely.

docs_links.title: Quickstart: Register an application with Microsoft Entra ID

label: Role Assignment
definition: An association between a security principal and a role definition at a given scope.
long_description: Role assignments bind users, groups, service principals, or managed identities to role definitions at subscription, resource group, or resource scope. For AI-300 and AI-103, they control who can manage ML workspaces, deploy models, access data, or invoke Azure OpenAI endpoints.

docs_links.title: What is Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC)?

label: Managed Identity
definition: An automatically managed identity for Azure resources to authenticate to services without secrets.
long_description: Managed identities (system-assigned or user-assigned) allow Azure resources like Azure Machine Learning compute, Functions, Container Apps, and AKS to authenticate to services such as Key Vault, Storage, Azure OpenAI, and Azure AI Search without storing credentials in code.

docs_links.title: What are managed identities for Azure resources?

label: Client Secret / Certificate Credential
definition: A credential associated with an app registration, used for confidential client authentication.
long_description: Client secrets and certificates are credentials attached to app registrations. In AI-300 and AI-103, they are sometimes used by external systems or CI/CD pipelines to call Azure APIs, though managed identities are preferred for in-Azure workloads.

docs_links.title: Add credentials to your app registration

label: Key Vault
definition: A secure store for secrets, keys, and certificates used by AI and ML workloads.
long_description: Azure Key Vault stores API keys, connection strings, certificates, and encryption keys. AI-300 and AI-103 solutions use Key Vault to store Azure OpenAI keys (when not using managed identity), storage keys, and other sensitive configuration for ML pipelines, agents, and applications.

label: Key Vault Secret
definition: A secret value stored in Azure Key Vault, such as an API key or connection string.
long_description: Key Vault secrets hold sensitive values like Azure OpenAI keys, database connection strings, or external API tokens. AI-300 and AI-103 workloads retrieve these secrets at runtime via managed identities or app credentials instead of hardcoding them.

label: Azure Machine Learning Workspace
definition: The top-level Azure Machine Learning resource that organizes ML assets and operations.
long_description: An Azure Machine Learning workspace is the central hub for managing data assets, compute, jobs, pipelines, models, environments, and endpoints. AI-300 focuses heavily on configuring, automating, and governing workspaces as part of MLOps practices.

label: Compute Cluster
definition: A scalable compute target used to run Azure ML training and batch inference jobs.
long_description: Compute clusters provide autoscaling CPU or GPU nodes for distributed training and batch inference. AI-300 scenarios include configuring clusters, attaching them to workspaces, and using them as targets for jobs and pipelines.

docs_links.title: Create and manage Azure Machine Learning compute clusters

label: Compute Instance
definition: A single-node development environment for interactive experimentation in Azure ML.
long_description: Compute instances are managed VMs with preconfigured environments for notebooks, VS Code, and CLI tools. They are used in AI-300 and AI-103 labs for authoring notebooks, jobs, and pipelines before automating them.

docs_links.title: Create and manage Azure Machine Learning compute instances

label: Environment
definition: A reusable definition of the software environment used to run ML jobs and deployments.
long_description: Environments encapsulate Conda dependencies, Docker images, and environment variables. AI-300 emphasizes versioning environments for reproducible training, scoring, and pipelines.

docs_links.title: Create and use software environments in Azure Machine Learning

label: Conda File
definition: A YAML file describing Conda dependencies for an Azure ML environment.
long_description: Conda environment specification files define Python packages and channels. They are referenced by Azure ML environments to ensure consistent dependencies across jobs, pipelines, and endpoints.

docs_links.title: Use Conda environments in Azure Machine Learning

label: Docker Image
definition: A container image used as the runtime environment for ML training and inference.
long_description: Docker images can be custom-built or curated by Azure ML. They define OS, drivers, and runtime dependencies for training jobs and deployed endpoints, which is critical for MLOps reproducibility in AI-300.

docs_links.title: Use Docker images with Azure Machine Learning

label: Data Asset
definition: A versioned reference to data used by ML jobs and pipelines.
long_description: Data assets point to data in Blob Storage, ADLS Gen2, or other sources. They are used by jobs and pipelines for training, validation, and batch scoring, and are central to reproducible ML workflows in AI-300.

docs_links.title: Create and use data assets in Azure Machine Learning

label: Job
definition: A unit of work submitted to Azure ML for training, evaluation, or batch inference.
long_description: Jobs encapsulate scripts, environments, inputs, and outputs. They run on compute targets and can be orchestrated by pipelines. In AI-300, jobs are the building blocks for automated training and evaluation workflows.

docs_links.title: Train models with Azure Machine Learning jobs

label: Pipeline
definition: A multi-step workflow that orchestrates jobs and components in Azure ML.
long_description: Pipelines define directed acyclic graphs of components that perform data preparation, training, evaluation, and deployment. AI-300 focuses on building, parameterizing, and automating pipelines as part of MLOps.

docs_links.title: Create and run machine learning pipelines

label: Pipeline Component
definition: A reusable step definition within an Azure ML pipeline.
long_description: Pipeline components encapsulate code, environment, and inputs/outputs for a single step. They promote reuse and versioning across pipelines, which is a key MLOps practice in AI-300.

docs_links.title: Create and use components in Azure Machine Learning pipelines

label: Azure AI Foundry Project
definition: A project in Azure AI Foundry that organizes agents, prompt flows, and AI resources.
long_description: Azure AI Foundry projects are containers for building generative AI applications and agents. AI-103 uses projects to manage connections to Azure OpenAI, AI Search, data sources, and tools, and to design multi-agent workflows.

label: Model
definition: A trained machine learning model artifact managed by Azure ML.
long_description: Models are registered artifacts produced by training jobs or pipelines. AI-300 covers registering, versioning, and promoting models across environments, as well as integrating them into endpoints and monitoring workflows.

docs_links.title: Register and manage models in Azure Machine Learning

label: Model Version
definition: A specific version of a registered model in Azure ML.
long_description: Each model can have multiple versions, representing different training runs or configurations. AI-300 scenarios include promoting specific versions to staging or production endpoints and rolling back if needed.

docs_links.title: Model versioning in Azure Machine Learning

label: Online Endpoint
definition: A managed online endpoint for real-time model inference.
long_description: Online endpoints expose models over HTTPS for low-latency scoring. AI-300 covers blue-green deployments, traffic splitting, and integration with monitoring and CI/CD pipelines.

docs_links.title: Deploy and score a machine learning model by using an online endpoint

label: Endpoint Deployment
definition: A specific deployment configuration of a model within an online endpoint.
long_description: Deployments define which model version, environment, and compute are used for a portion of endpoint traffic. AI-300 includes configuring multiple deployments for canary releases and A/B testing.

label: Batch Endpoint
definition: An endpoint for running large-scale batch inference jobs.
long_description: Batch endpoints orchestrate batch scoring over large datasets using compute clusters. AI-300 scenarios include configuring input/output locations, scaling, and monitoring batch runs.

docs_links.title: Deploy and score models with batch endpoints

label: Responsible AI Dashboard
definition: A dashboard for analyzing model fairness, explainability, and error patterns.
long_description: The Responsible AI dashboard helps practitioners understand model behavior, feature importance, and fairness metrics. AI-300 includes configuring and interpreting this dashboard as part of responsible ML practices.

docs_links.title: Responsible AI dashboard in Azure Machine Learning

label: MLflow Tracking Server
definition: The MLflow tracking backend integrated with Azure Machine Learning.
long_description: Azure ML integrates MLflow for experiment tracking. Jobs and pipelines log parameters, metrics, and artifacts to the tracking server, enabling comparison of runs and traceability of models used in AI-300 workflows.

docs_links.title: Track ML experiments with MLflow and Azure Machine Learning

label: MLflow Experiment
definition: A logical group of MLflow runs representing related experiments.
long_description: MLflow experiments group runs for a particular model or scenario. AI-300 uses experiments to organize training runs, compare metrics, and select candidate models for registration.

docs_links.title: MLflow experiments in Azure Machine Learning

label: MLflow Run
definition: A single execution record in MLflow with parameters, metrics, and artifacts.
long_description: Each MLflow run corresponds to a training or evaluation execution. Runs log metrics and artifacts that can be associated with registered models, providing traceability required in AI-300 MLOps practices.

docs_links.title: MLflow runs in Azure Machine Learning

label: Azure OpenAI Connection
definition: A connection from Azure AI Foundry to an Azure OpenAI resource and deployment.
long_description: Azure AI Foundry uses connections to bind projects to Azure OpenAI resources and specific model deployments. AI-103 uses these connections to invoke GPT models from agents and prompt flows.

docs_links.title: Connect to Azure OpenAI in Azure AI Foundry

label: Tokenizer
definition: A component that splits text into tokens for model input.
long_description: Tokenizers convert raw text into model-compatible tokens using byte-pair encoding, sentencepiece, or unigram models. They ensure consistent input formatting for LLMs, embedding models, and fine-tuning pipelines. Azure AI Foundry uses tokenizers in preprocessing, RAG pipelines, and evaluation workflows.

label: Text Chunker
definition: A component that splits documents into smaller segments for embedding and retrieval.
long_description: Text chunkers divide long documents into semantically meaningful segments using fixed-size, sliding window, or semantic chunking strategies. Chunking improves retrieval accuracy and reduces hallucinations in RAG pipelines. Azure AI Foundry integrates chunkers into data ingestion workflows.

docs_links.title: Text Split Skill (Chunking) in Azure AI Search

label: Embedding Pipeline
definition: A pipeline that generates embeddings from text or images for indexing and retrieval.
long_description: Embedding pipelines combine tokenization, chunking, embedding model inference, and vector storage. They prepare data for RAG systems, semantic search, and agent memory. Azure AI Foundry supports embedding pipelines through Prompt Flow and data ingestion workflows.

docs_links.title: Integrated vectorization in Azure AI Search

label: Document Parser
definition: A component that extracts structured text from documents such as PDFs, Word files, or HTML.
long_description: Document parsers convert unstructured files into clean text suitable for chunking and embedding. They handle layout extraction, table parsing, and metadata extraction. Azure Document Intelligence and custom parsers are commonly used in RAG pipelines.

label: OCR Preprocessor
definition: A preprocessing step that prepares images or scanned documents for OCR.
long_description: OCR preprocessors enhance image quality, correct skew, remove noise, and segment text regions. They improve OCR accuracy for scanned documents and images. Azure AI Vision and Document Intelligence integrate OCR preprocessing into document workflows.

docs_links.title: OCR with Azure AI Document Intelligence

label: Data Labeling Project
definition: A project for annotating text, images, or audio for training or evaluation.
long_description: Data labeling projects support annotation of entities, classifications, bounding boxes, and preference pairs. They are used for fine-tuning, alignment, and evaluation datasets. Azure ML and third-party tools integrate with labeling workflows.

docs_links.title: Data labeling projects in Azure Machine Learning

label: Annotation Tool
definition: A tool used to annotate datasets for training or evaluation.
long_description: Annotation tools support labeling tasks such as NER, sentiment, image tagging, and preference ranking. They integrate with Azure ML datasets and can export data for fine-tuning, DPO, and evaluation pipelines.

docs_links.title: Data labeling tool in Azure Machine Learning

label: Dataset Validator
definition: A component that checks datasets for quality, consistency, and formatting issues.
long_description: Dataset validators detect missing values, formatting errors, label inconsistencies, and safety violations. They ensure datasets meet quality standards before fine-tuning or evaluation. Azure AI Foundry integrates validation into data ingestion workflows.

docs_links.title: Validation Activity in Azure Data Factory

label: Data Cleaning Pipeline
definition: A pipeline that removes noise, normalizes text, and prepares data for modeling.
long_description: Data cleaning pipelines perform normalization, deduplication, stopword removal, text correction, and safety filtering. They ensure high-quality inputs for embedding, fine-tuning, and evaluation. Azure AI Foundry supports cleaning pipelines through Prompt Flow and custom scripts.

docs_links.title: Data cleaning with dataflows in Microsoft Fabric

label: Text Normalizer
definition: A component that standardizes text formatting and structure.
long_description: Text normalizers perform lowercasing, punctuation cleanup, whitespace normalization, and Unicode correction. They ensure consistent input for tokenizers, embedding models, and fine-tuning pipelines.

docs_links.title: Text normalization for filters, facets, and sorting in Azure AI Search

label: Text Filter
definition: A component that removes unwanted or unsafe text segments.
long_description: Text filters remove profanity, PII, boilerplate content, or irrelevant sections. They are used in safety preprocessing, dataset cleaning, and RAG ingestion workflows.

docs_links.title: Filters in Azure AI Search queries

label: Metadata Extractor
definition: A component that extracts metadata such as titles, authors, timestamps, and tags.
long_description: Metadata extractors enrich documents with structured metadata used for filtering, ranking, and retrieval. They support RAG pipelines, document indexing, and dataset management.

docs_links.title: Create an extractor for document processing (Document Intelligence)

label: Document Embedding Store
definition: A storage system for embeddings and metadata used in retrieval pipelines.
long_description: Document embedding stores combine vectors, metadata, and chunk references. They support semantic search, hybrid retrieval, and agent memory. Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB, and pgvector are commonly used embedding stores.

docs_links.title: Azure OpenAI embeddings store output binding for Azure Functions

label: Data Ingestion Pipeline
definition: A pipeline that ingests, processes, and indexes data for AI applications.
long_description: Data ingestion pipelines orchestrate parsing, cleaning, chunking, embedding, and indexing. They prepare data for RAG, search, and agent workflows. Azure AI Foundry integrates ingestion pipelines with Prompt Flow and vector stores.

docs_links.title: Data ingestion pipelines in Azure Machine Learning

label: Data Transformation Component
definition: A component that transforms data into model-ready formats.
long_description: Data transformation components perform feature extraction, normalization, enrichment, and schema mapping. They are used in ML pipelines, RAG ingestion, and evaluation workflows.

docs_links.title: Mapping data flows in Azure Data Factory

label: Data Quality Monitor
definition: A component that monitors data quality over time.
long_description: Data quality monitors track drift, anomalies, missing values, and schema changes. They ensure long-term reliability of RAG pipelines, fine-tuning datasets, and evaluation workflows.

docs_links.title: Data quality monitoring in Azure Databricks

label: Preprocessing Pipeline
definition: A pipeline that prepares raw data for modeling, retrieval, or evaluation.
long_description: Preprocessing pipelines combine cleaning, normalization, filtering, and transformation steps. They ensure consistent, high-quality inputs for embedding, fine-tuning, and evaluation. Azure AI Foundry supports preprocessing pipelines through Prompt Flow and Azure ML.

docs_links.title: Preprocessing pipelines in Azure Machine Learning

label: Data Pipeline Run
definition: A single execution instance of a data processing pipeline.
long_description: Data pipeline runs track execution metadata, logs, metrics, and outputs. They support reproducibility and debugging for ingestion, preprocessing, and embedding workflows. Azure AI Foundry logs pipeline runs for monitoring and governance.

docs_links.title: Pipeline runs in Azure Data Factory

label: Data Pipeline Artifact
definition: An output artifact produced by a data pipeline run.
long_description: Pipeline artifacts include cleaned datasets, embeddings, metadata files, and logs. They are versioned and stored for reuse in RAG pipelines, fine-tuning jobs, and evaluation workflows.

label: Data Catalog Entry
definition: A cataloged reference to a dataset, embedding store, or pipeline artifact.
long_description: Data catalog entries store metadata, lineage, and versioning information for datasets and artifacts. They support governance, discoverability, and reproducibility in Azure AI Foundry and Azure ML.

docs_links.title: Azure Data Catalog (now part of Microsoft Purview)

label: Agent Orchestrator
definition: A coordination engine that manages multi-agent workflows and execution.
long_description: The Agent Orchestrator routes tasks, manages agent lifecycles, and coordinates tool usage across complex agentic systems. It is responsible for delegating subtasks, merging outputs, and ensuring consistent state across agents.

label: Agent Router
definition: A routing component that selects the appropriate agent or tool for a given task.
long_description: The Agent Router evaluates task metadata, context, and policies to determine which agent or tool should handle a request. It supports dynamic routing, fallback strategies, and multi-agent delegation.

label: Agent Policy Engine
definition: A policy enforcement engine that governs agent behavior and tool usage.
long_description: The Agent Policy Engine enforces safety, compliance, and operational rules for agent execution. It evaluates constraints, validates tool calls, and ensures that agent actions adhere to organizational policies.

docs_links.title: Azure Policy for Open Policy Agent (OPA)

label: Agent Execution Trace
definition: A detailed log of agent reasoning, tool calls, and decision paths.
long_description: Execution traces capture the full chain of thought, tool invocations, intermediate states, and final outputs of agent workflows. They are essential for debugging, auditing, and compliance.

docs_links.title: Trace and observe AI agents in Azure AI Foundry

label: Agent Session
definition: A persistent conversational or task session for an agent.
long_description: Agent Sessions maintain context, memory, and state across multiple interactions. They support long-running tasks, multi-step reasoning, and persistent agent identity.

docs_links.title: Agent Framework – AgentSession for conversation context

label: Agent State Store
definition: A storage layer for agent state, context variables, and working memory.
long_description: The Agent State Store maintains short-term and long-term memory, including variables, intermediate results, and contextual embeddings. It enables continuity across agent tasks.

label: Agent Knowledge Base
definition: A structured knowledge repository used by agents for reasoning and retrieval.
long_description: The Agent Knowledge Base stores documents, embeddings, structured facts, and domain-specific knowledge. It integrates with vector stores, RAG pipelines, and graph-based retrieval.

docs_links.title: Knowledge base for agents (Azure AI Search)

label: Agent Tool Registry
definition: A registry of tools available to agents for execution.
long_description: The Agent Tool Registry defines available tools, schemas, authentication, and invocation rules. It ensures consistent tool usage across multi-agent systems.

docs_links.title: Integrate agent tools using an MCP server

label: Agent Tool Schema
definition: A structured schema describing tool inputs, outputs, and invocation rules.
long_description: Tool Schemas define the interface for agent tools, including parameter types, validation rules, and expected outputs. They enable safe and predictable tool execution.

docs_links.title: OpenAPI schema integration with Foundry Agent Service

label: Browser Tool
definition: A tool enabling agents to browse web pages, extract content, and follow links.
long_description: The Browser Tool allows agents to perform web navigation, retrieve page content, and extract structured information. It is commonly used for research, fact-checking, and dynamic data retrieval.

docs_links.title: Browser Automation Tool for Foundry agents

label: Code Interpreter Tool
definition: A tool that executes Python code for data analysis, transformation, and computation.
long_description: The Code Interpreter Tool provides a secure sandbox for executing Python code. Agents use it for data cleaning, visualization, statistical analysis, and generating structured outputs.

label: SQL Query Tool
definition: A tool that allows agents to execute SQL queries against relational databases.
long_description: The SQL Query Tool enables agents to interact with Azure SQL, PostgreSQL, and other relational stores. It supports read-only queries, schema inspection, and safe parameterized execution.

docs_links.title: Query Editor in Azure Portal (SQL queries)

label: Graph Query Tool
definition: A tool enabling agents to query graph databases and knowledge graphs.
long_description: The Graph Query Tool supports Cypher, Gremlin, and GraphRAG-style queries. It allows agents to traverse relationships, extract structured knowledge, and perform graph reasoning.

label: Vector Search Tool
definition: A tool that performs vector similarity search across embeddings.
long_description: The Vector Search Tool integrates with Azure AI Search, pgvector, Redis, Pinecone, and Milvus. It enables agents to retrieve semantically similar documents, images, or embeddings.

docs_links.title: Vector Search in Azure AI Search

label: API Connector Tool
definition: A tool that allows agents to call external APIs using structured schemas.
long_description: The API Connector Tool defines API endpoints, authentication, rate limits, and schemas. Agents use it to integrate with SaaS systems, internal APIs, and cloud services.

docs_links.title: API connectors in Azure AD B2C

label: Automation Tool
definition: A tool enabling agents to trigger workflows in Power Automate or Logic Apps.
long_description: The Automation Tool allows agents to orchestrate business workflows, send notifications, update records, and automate multi-step processes across enterprise systems.

label: Azure AI Orchestration Flow
definition: A workflow engine for coordinating AI tasks, agents, and tools.
long_description: Azure AI Orchestration Flow defines multi-step workflows that integrate models, agents, tools, and data sources. It supports branching, triggers, retries, and monitoring.

docs_links.title: Orchestration workflow in Azure AI Language

label: AI Orchestration Step
definition: A single step within an AI orchestration workflow.
long_description: Orchestration Steps represent atomic operations such as model inference, agent invocation, data transformation, or tool execution. They support parallelism and conditional logic.

docs_links.title: Orchestration workflow – step definition and patterns

label: AI Orchestration Trigger
definition: A trigger that starts an AI orchestration workflow.
long_description: Triggers include HTTP requests, scheduled events, data updates, or agent actions. They initiate orchestration flows and pass initial context to downstream steps.

docs_links.title: Triggers in Azure AI orchestration (Logic Apps)

label: AI Safety Guardrail
definition: A configurable safety rule that restricts model or agent behavior.
long_description: AI Safety Guardrails enforce constraints on model outputs, tool usage, and agent actions. They help organizations ensure compliance, reduce risk, and maintain safe operational boundaries.

docs_links.title: Guardrails and controls in Azure AI

label: AI Safety Policy
definition: A policy document defining safety rules, constraints, and enforcement logic.
long_description: AI Safety Policies define allowed and disallowed behaviors for models and agents. They include content filters, tool restrictions, compliance rules, and escalation procedures.

label: AI Safety Evaluation Report
definition: A report summarizing safety test results, violations, and mitigation recommendations.
long_description: Safety Evaluation Reports document the outcomes of automated and manual safety tests. They include violation logs, severity scoring, and recommended guardrail adjustments.

label: Weaviate Vector Store
definition: A cloud-native vector database optimized for semantic search and hybrid retrieval.
long_description: Weaviate provides vector indexing, hybrid search, schema-based storage, and modular retrieval pipelines. It integrates with Azure AI Search and GraphRAG architectures.

docs_links.title: Weaviate vector store connector in Semantic Kernel

label: Qdrant Vector Store
definition: A high-performance vector database supporting HNSW indexing and payload filtering.
long_description: Qdrant enables fast vector search with metadata filtering, distributed clustering, and hybrid retrieval. It is widely used in RAG and agentic systems.

docs_links.title: Qdrant vector store connector in Semantic Kernel

label: FAISS Index
definition: A local vector index library optimized for similarity search and clustering.
long_description: FAISS provides GPU-accelerated vector search and supports IVF, HNSW, and PQ indexing. It is commonly used for offline RAG pipelines and embedding clustering.

docs_links.title: FAISS vector index in Azure Machine Learning

label: Elastic Vector Search
definition: A vector-enabled search engine built on Elasticsearch.
long_description: Elastic Vector Search combines BM25, dense embeddings, and hybrid scoring. It supports large-scale document retrieval and enterprise-grade observability.

docs_links.title: Elastic vector search in Azure AI Search

label: OpenSearch Vector Index
definition: A vector search engine built on the OpenSearch platform.
long_description: OpenSearch Vector Index supports ANN search, hybrid scoring, and distributed indexing. It integrates with RAG pipelines and enterprise search workloads.

docs_links.title: Vector index in Azure AI Search (OpenSearch compatibility)

label: GraphRAG Knowledge Graph
definition: A knowledge graph used to enhance retrieval-augmented generation.
long_description: GraphRAG Knowledge Graphs store entities, relationships, and embeddings. They enable structured retrieval, reasoning, and multi-hop question answering.

docs_links.title: GraphRAG with Azure Database for PostgreSQL

label: GraphRAG Query Engine
definition: A query engine that retrieves structured and unstructured knowledge from GraphRAG stores.
long_description: The GraphRAG Query Engine performs graph traversal, embedding search, and hybrid retrieval. It supports multi-hop reasoning and contextual grounding for LLMs.

docs_links.title: GraphRAG query execution (openCypher and vector search)

label: Prompt Guardrail
definition: A rule-based constraint applied to prompts to prevent unsafe or undesired model behavior.
long_description: Prompt Guardrails enforce safety, compliance, and operational constraints before a prompt is sent to a model. They filter harmful content, block restricted topics, and ensure adherence to organizational policies.

docs_links.title: Prompt Shields in Azure AI Content Safety

label: Prompt Policy
definition: A policy document defining allowed prompt patterns, constraints, and safety rules.
long_description: Prompt Policies define organizational rules for prompt construction, including restricted terms, required disclaimers, and safety checks. They ensure consistent and compliant prompt engineering.

docs_links.title: Azure Policy definitions for prompts (MCP Server policy tool)

label: Prompt Template Version
definition: A versioned snapshot of a prompt template used for reproducible inference.
long_description: Prompt Template Versions allow teams to track changes, roll back to previous configurations, and ensure reproducibility across deployments and evaluations.

label: Prompt Optimization Pipeline
definition: A pipeline that automatically optimizes prompts for accuracy, safety, and cost.
long_description: Prompt Optimization Pipelines evaluate multiple prompt variants, measure performance metrics, and select the best-performing prompt for deployment. They support automated tuning and A/B testing.

docs_links.title: Tune prompts using variants in prompt flow

label: Prompt Optimization Report
definition: A report summarizing prompt performance, evaluation metrics, and recommended improvements.
long_description: Prompt Optimization Reports include accuracy metrics, safety scores, latency measurements, and cost analysis. They guide teams in selecting optimal prompt configurations.

docs_links.title: Evaluation and metrics in prompt flow

label: Azure ML Serverless Compute
definition: A serverless compute option for running ML inference without managing infrastructure.
long_description: Azure ML Serverless Compute automatically provisions and scales compute resources for model inference. It supports autoscaling, GPU acceleration, and cost-efficient execution.

docs_links.title: Model training on serverless compute in Azure Machine Learning

label: Inference Autoscaling Profile
definition: A configuration defining autoscaling rules for ML inference workloads.
long_description: Autoscaling Profiles define scaling thresholds, concurrency limits, and resource allocation strategies for ML endpoints. They ensure optimal performance and cost efficiency.

docs_links.title: Autoscaling online endpoints in Azure Machine Learning

label: Model Serving Pool
definition: A shared pool of compute resources for hosting multiple ML models.
long_description: Model Serving Pools allow multiple models to share GPU or CPU resources, improving utilization and reducing cost. They support dynamic model loading and eviction.

docs_links.title: Model Serving pools in Azure Databricks

label: Model Serving Configuration
definition: A configuration defining how models are loaded, cached, and executed in serving pools.
long_description: Model Serving Configurations specify memory allocation, batching strategies, concurrency limits, and model lifecycle rules for shared serving environments.

docs_links.title: Model Serving configuration in Azure Databricks

label: AI Gateway
definition: A secure gateway for routing AI inference traffic across services and regions.
long_description: AI Gateways provide traffic management, authentication, rate limiting, and routing for AI workloads. They integrate with Azure Front Door, API Management, and private networking.

label: Private Link Hub
definition: A centralized hub for managing private endpoints across AI services.
long_description: Private Link Hubs simplify the management of private endpoints for Azure ML, Azure OpenAI, AI Search, and vector databases. They improve security posture and reduce configuration complexity.

docs_links.title: Private Link in a hub-and-spoke network

label: AI Governance Policy
definition: A governance document defining rules for AI usage, safety, and compliance.
long_description: AI Governance Policies define organizational standards for model usage, data handling, agent behavior, and risk mitigation. They ensure responsible and compliant AI operations.

docs_links.title: AI governance with Azure Policy and Azure AI Foundry

label: AI Audit Log
definition: A log capturing model inferences, agent actions, and tool executions for auditing.
long_description: AI Audit Logs provide traceability for AI systems, including prompts, outputs, tool calls, and safety violations. They support compliance, forensics, and monitoring.

docs_links.title: Audit logs for Azure AI services (Azure Monitor)

label: MAI Model Catalog
definition: A centralized catalog of Microsoft AI models, variants, and capabilities.
long_description: The MAI Model Catalog provides discovery, metadata, versioning, and deployment options for MAI models. It includes model families, variants, adapters, and safety configurations.

docs_links.title: MAI-Image model catalog in Microsoft Foundry

label: MAI Model Registry
definition: A registry storing MAI model versions, metadata, and deployment configurations.
long_description: The MAI Model Registry manages model lineage, versioning, adapters, and deployment metadata. It integrates with Azure ML, Azure AI Foundry, and MAI Runtime.

docs_links.title: Model registration in Azure Machine Learning

label: MAI Model Variant
definition: A specialized version of a MAI model optimized for specific tasks or modalities.
long_description: MAI Model Variants include task‑optimized, domain‑specific, and resource‑optimized versions of MAI‑1 models. They support fine‑tuning, adapters, and safety layers.

label: MAI Model Adapter
definition: A lightweight adapter enabling efficient fine‑tuning of MAI models.
long_description: MAI Model Adapters support LoRA, QLoRA, and task‑specific parameter‑efficient tuning. They allow organizations to customize MAI models without retraining the full model.

label: MAI Runtime
definition: The execution environment for MAI models, agents, and tools.
long_description: MAI Runtime provides optimized inference, batching, caching, and safety enforcement for MAI models. It integrates with Azure ML, Azure AI Foundry, and MAI Safety Layers.

label: MAI Safety Layer
definition: A safety enforcement layer applied to MAI model inference and agent actions.
long_description: The MAI Safety Layer applies guardrails, filters, and policy checks to model outputs and tool calls. It ensures safe, compliant, and controlled AI behavior.

docs_links.title: Guardrails and controls (MAI safety layer overview)

label: MAI System Configuration
definition: A configuration defining runtime, safety, and resource settings for MAI systems.
long_description: MAI System Configurations define memory allocation, safety rules, adapter loading, and runtime behavior for MAI deployments across cloud and edge environments.

label: MAI‑1 Base
definition: The base version of the MAI‑1 model family.
long_description: MAI‑1 Base is a general‑purpose foundation model supporting text, reasoning, and multimodal extensions. It serves as the core for MAI‑1 variants and adapters.

label: MAI‑1 Instruct
definition: An instruction‑tuned version of MAI‑1 optimized for conversational and agentic tasks.
long_description: MAI‑1 Instruct is fine‑tuned for instruction following, reasoning, and agent workflows. It includes safety tuning and improved tool‑use capabilities.

label: MAI‑1 Reasoning
definition: A reasoning‑optimized MAI‑1 variant for complex problem solving.
long_description: MAI‑1 Reasoning enhances chain‑of‑thought, multi‑step planning, and symbolic reasoning. It is used in agentic systems requiring high‑precision decision making.

docs_links.title: MAI-1 Reasoning (in the context of MAI model family)

label: MAI‑1 Vision
definition: A multimodal MAI‑1 variant optimized for image understanding and visual reasoning.
long_description: MAI‑1 Vision supports image captioning, object detection, OCR, visual question answering, and multimodal embeddings. It integrates with Azure AI Vision and MAI Runtime.

label: MAI‑1 Audio
definition: An audio‑optimized MAI‑1 variant supporting speech recognition and synthesis.
long_description: MAI‑1 Audio provides speech‑to‑text, text‑to‑speech, speaker identification, and audio embeddings. It integrates with Azure AI Speech and MAI Runtime.

label: MAI‑1 Mini
definition: A compact MAI‑1 model variant optimized for edge and low‑latency workloads.
long_description: MAI‑1 Mini is designed for mobile, embedded, and low‑resource environments. It supports text, reasoning, and lightweight multimodal tasks.

label: MAI‑1 Small
definition: A small MAI‑1 variant optimized for cost‑efficient inference.
long_description: MAI‑1 Small balances performance and efficiency, supporting enterprise workloads, chatbots, and agentic reasoning with reduced compute requirements.

label: MAI‑1 Medium
definition: A mid‑sized MAI‑1 variant offering strong reasoning and multimodal capabilities.
long_description: MAI‑1 Medium is suitable for enterprise agents, RAG systems, and multimodal applications requiring balanced performance and cost.

label: MAI‑1 Large
definition: A large MAI‑1 variant optimized for advanced reasoning and multimodal tasks.
long_description: MAI‑1 Large provides state‑of‑the‑art reasoning, planning, and multimodal understanding. It powers complex agentic systems and enterprise AI applications.

label: Agent Execution Sandbox
definition: A secure sandbox environment for executing agent code and tool calls.
long_description: The Agent Execution Sandbox isolates agent operations, ensuring safe execution of code, tool invocations, and external API calls. It enforces resource limits and security boundaries.

label: Agent Execution Policy
definition: A policy defining allowed operations, tools, and resource limits for agents.
long_description: Agent Execution Policies specify which tools agents may use, allowed API domains, rate limits, and safety constraints. They ensure controlled and compliant agent behavior.

docs_links.title: Execution modes and approval policies in Azure SRE Agent

label: RAG Cache
definition: A caching layer that stores retrieval results to reduce repeated vector searches.
long_description: RAG Cache improves performance by storing embeddings, retrieved documents, and reranker outputs. It reduces latency and cost for repeated queries.

docs_links.title: Semantic cache for RAG (Azure Cosmos DB)

label: RAG Query Planner
definition: A planner that determines the optimal retrieval strategy for a query.
long_description: The RAG Query Planner selects between vector search, hybrid search, graph traversal, or multi‑step retrieval based on query intent and context.

docs_links.title: RAG query planning and execution (Azure Databricks RAG chain)

label: RAG Fusion Engine
definition: A fusion engine that merges results from multiple retrieval strategies.
long_description: The RAG Fusion Engine combines vector search, keyword search, graph retrieval, and reranking outputs to produce the most relevant context for LLMs.

label: MAI Agent Runtime
definition: The execution environment for MAI-native agents, tools, and workflows.
long_description: The MAI Agent Runtime provides optimized execution for agent reasoning, tool calling, memory management, and safety enforcement. It integrates deeply with MAI models and MAI Runtime.

docs_links.title: MAI Agent Runtime (Agent Framework runtime overview)

label: MAI Agent Container
definition: A containerized execution unit for MAI agents and their toolchains.
long_description: MAI Agent Containers package agent logic, tools, memory modules, and safety layers into isolated execution units. They support scaling, versioning, and reproducibility.

docs_links.title: Agent containerization (Azure Container Apps for agents)

label: MAI Agent Memory Store
definition: A unified memory store for MAI agents, supporting vector, episodic, and tool memory.
long_description: The MAI Agent Memory Store provides structured and unstructured memory for MAI agents. It supports embeddings, episodic logs, tool results, and long-term knowledge retention.

docs_links.title: MAI Agent Memory Store (Agent Framework memory)

label: MAI Policy Engine
definition: A policy engine enforcing safety, compliance, and operational rules for MAI systems.
long_description: The MAI Policy Engine evaluates safety rules, compliance constraints, and operational limits for MAI models and agents. It integrates with MAI Safety Layer and Azure governance tools.

docs_links.title: Policy Engine in Microsoft Information Protection (MIP) SDK

label: MAI Guardrail Engine
definition: A guardrail engine applying safety filters and constraints to MAI model outputs.
long_description: The MAI Guardrail Engine enforces content filters, tool restrictions, and safety rules. It ensures safe and compliant behavior across MAI models and agent workflows.

label: MAI Evaluation Suite
definition: A suite of tools for evaluating MAI models, agents, and workflows.
long_description: The MAI Evaluation Suite provides automated tests for accuracy, safety, robustness, latency, and cost. It integrates with Azure ML evaluation pipelines and MAI Runtime.

docs_links.title: Evaluation Suite in Azure AI Foundry

label: MAI Evaluation Report
definition: A report summarizing evaluation metrics for MAI models and agents.
long_description: MAI Evaluation Reports include accuracy, safety, robustness, and performance metrics. They support model selection, deployment decisions, and compliance documentation.

docs_links.title: Evaluation reports in Azure AI Foundry

label: GraphRAG Context Optimizer
definition: A component that optimizes retrieved graph context for LLM consumption.
long_description: The GraphRAG Context Optimizer selects the most relevant nodes, edges, and embeddings from a knowledge graph to construct high‑quality context windows for LLMs.

label: GraphRAG Fusion Engine
definition: A fusion engine that merges graph‑based and vector‑based retrieval results.
long_description: The GraphRAG Fusion Engine combines graph traversal outputs, vector search results, and semantic ranking to produce optimal context for LLM reasoning.

label: Model Drift Detector
definition: A detector that identifies changes in model behavior over time.
long_description: Model Drift Detectors monitor prediction distributions, feature drift, and performance degradation. They trigger alerts and retraining workflows when drift is detected.

docs_links.title: Model drift detection in Azure Machine Learning

label: Embedding Drift Monitor
definition: A monitor that detects drift in embedding distributions for RAG and agent systems.
long_description: Embedding Drift Monitors track changes in embedding clusters, semantic distributions, and retrieval quality. They help maintain RAG accuracy and agent reliability.

label: Prompt Drift Monitor
definition: A monitor that detects changes in prompt patterns affecting model performance.
long_description: Prompt Drift Monitors track shifts in prompt structure, intent, and semantics. They help maintain model accuracy and prevent degradation caused by evolving user behavior.

docs_links.title: Plan drift detection and critic agents

label: Safety Drift Monitor
definition: A monitor that detects changes in model safety behavior over time.
long_description: Safety Drift Monitors identify shifts in toxicity, bias, hallucination rates, and policy violations. They ensure long-term safety compliance for deployed AI systems.

docs_links.title: Safety drift detection (Guardrails and critic agents)

label: RAG Rewriter
definition: A component that rewrites user queries to improve retrieval accuracy.
long_description: The RAG Rewriter reformulates ambiguous or incomplete queries into optimized forms for vector, hybrid, or graph retrieval. It improves recall and reduces irrelevant results.

label: RAG Context Window Manager
definition: A component that optimizes context window composition for LLM inference.
long_description: The RAG Context Window Manager selects, compresses, and arranges retrieved content to maximize relevance within model token limits. It supports chunk ranking, summarization, and fusion.

label: Agent Collaboration Protocol
definition: A protocol defining how multiple agents communicate and coordinate.
long_description: The Agent Collaboration Protocol defines message formats, delegation rules, conflict resolution, and shared memory access for multi-agent systems.

label: Agent Delegation Engine
definition: An engine that delegates tasks between agents based on skills and context.
long_description: The Agent Delegation Engine evaluates agent capabilities, workload, and context to assign tasks dynamically. It supports hierarchical, peer-to-peer, and supervisor delegation models.

label: AI Compliance Profile
definition: A compliance configuration defining regulatory requirements for AI systems.
long_description: AI Compliance Profiles define GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, and industry-specific requirements for AI deployments. They integrate with audit logs, safety layers, and governance policies.

label: AI Risk Assessment
definition: An assessment identifying risks associated with AI models and agents.
long_description: AI Risk Assessments evaluate safety, fairness, robustness, privacy, and operational risks. They guide mitigation strategies and compliance decisions.

docs_links.title: Risk and safety evaluators for generative AI (classic)

label: AI Usage Policy
definition: A policy defining acceptable use of AI systems within an organization.
long_description: AI Usage Policies specify allowed applications, prohibited behaviors, data handling rules, and user responsibilities. They ensure responsible and ethical AI adoption.

label: Model Fact Sheet
definition: A documentation artifact describing a model’s purpose, limitations, and risks.
long_description: Model Fact Sheets include intended use cases, known limitations, training data summaries, safety considerations, and evaluation metrics. They support transparency and governance.

docs_links.title: Model Fact Sheet (part of Azure AI governance)

label: Model Safety Card
definition: A safety-focused documentation artifact describing model risks and mitigations.
long_description: Model Safety Cards document safety evaluations, risk categories, mitigation strategies, and known failure modes. They are required for regulated AI deployments.

docs_links.title: Model Safety Card (part of Azure AI governance)

label: Microsoft Foundry
definition: A unified Azure PaaS offering for enterprise AI operations — model deployment, agent building, evaluation, and governance under a single resource provider.
long_description: Microsoft Foundry (rebranded from Azure AI Foundry at Microsoft Ignite November 2025) is the top-level platform that unifies agents, models, and tools under one Azure resource provider namespace (microsoft.foundry). It provides production-grade infrastructure with a portal, unified RBAC, networking, policy, tracing, monitoring, and evaluations. It replaces the older Hub + Azure OpenAI + Azure AI Services model with a single Foundry resource containing projects. The Foundry API offers a consistent contract for agentic applications across model providers, complemented by SDKs (azure-ai-projects 2.x) and a VS Code extension.

label: Foundry Resource
definition: The top-level Azure resource in Microsoft Foundry that scopes governance, model deployments, agent service, evaluations, and enterprise controls.
long_description: A Foundry Resource (formerly Azure AI Services, microsoft.foundry/accounts) is the single Azure ARM object that replaces the older Hub + Azure OpenAI + Azure AI Services trio. It manages governance settings (networking, security, RBAC, Azure Policy), model deployments (serverless and provisioned), the Foundry Agent Service endpoint, and connected Azure services such as Storage, Key Vault, and AI Search. Projects are created inside a Foundry Resource and inherit its governance boundaries. Azure OpenAI resources can be upgraded to Foundry Resources while preserving endpoints and API keys.

label: Foundry Project
definition: A development boundary inside a Foundry Resource where teams build, evaluate, and iterate on AI use cases, isolated from other projects.
long_description: A Foundry Project is a logical container for AI development work — files, agents, evaluations, model deployments, and connections — scoped within a Foundry Resource. Multiple projects share the resource's model deployments and connections without requiring repeated IT setup. Projects are the unit of access management for developers. They replace the older hub-based project concept and are accessible via the new Foundry portal, the azure-ai-projects 2.x SDK, and the Foundry API endpoint (https://<resource>.ai.azure.com/api/projects/<project>).

label: Foundry Project Asset
definition: A file, agent, evaluation, connection, or artifact scoped to a Foundry Project, managed under the project's governance boundary.
long_description: Project assets include uploaded files, agent definitions, evaluation runs, prompt templates, model deployments, vector store connections, and monitoring outputs. They inherit the security and compliance settings of the parent Foundry Resource while being isolated to the project scope. Assets are versioned and traceable, supporting MLOps and responsible AI practices.

label: Foundry AI Hub (Classic)
definition: The older hub-based resource type (built on Azure ML) that provides open-source model hosting, fine-tuning, and managed compute — accessible via the Foundry classic portal.
long_description: Foundry AI Hub (microsoft.machinelearningservices/workspaces kind=Hub) is the predecessor architecture to the Foundry Resource. It bundles an Azure ML workspace with AI model capabilities, supports managed compute for Hugging Face models, and requires a linked Foundry Resource. New investments are focused on the Foundry Resource type. Prompt Flow and Agents v1 (classic) are available here but are deprecated (retirement 2027). Hub-based projects can connect to a Foundry Resource to use its model deployments.

label: Foundry API
definition: A unified REST API for building agentic applications across model providers, using the Responses API (v1 stable routes).
long_description: The Foundry API provides a consistent contract for agentic application development, replacing the older Assistants API (Agents v1). It exposes stable v1 routes (/openai/v1/) via project endpoints, supports the Responses API for multi-turn agent interactions, and is backed by the azure-ai-projects 2.x SDK. It integrates with MCP and A2A protocols for enterprise agent interoperability.

label: Foundry Model Catalog
definition: A catalog of 1,900+ models from Microsoft, OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Meta, DeepSeek, xAI, and Hugging Face, browsable and deployable from the Foundry portal.
long_description: The Foundry Model Catalog provides discovery, metadata, deployment options, and safety cards for all supported models. Models can be deployed via serverless API (Foundry resource), provisioned throughput (PTU), or managed compute (Hub/AML). It includes model families GPT-5, GPT-4.1, Claude, Grok, Mistral, DeepSeek-R1, Phi-4, Llama, MAI, and many others. Safety cards and fact sheets are available for each model.

label: Foundry IQ
definition: A managed knowledge layer that turns enterprise data into reusable, permission-aware knowledge bases for AI agents.
long_description: Foundry IQ enables creation of multi-source knowledge bases that provide agents with permission-aware responses based on organizational data. A knowledge base consists of knowledge sources (Azure Blob Storage, SharePoint, OneLake, public web) and retrieval parameters. Multiple agents can share the same knowledge base. Foundry IQ uses agentic retrieval — LLM-driven query decomposition, parallel subquery execution, semantic reranking, and citation-backed responses. It automates chunking, vector embedding generation, metadata extraction, and incremental indexer scheduling. Azure AI Search provides the underlying indexing and retrieval infrastructure.

label: Foundry IQ Knowledge Base
definition: A top-level resource orchestrating agentic retrieval — defines knowledge sources to query and retrieval behavior (reasoning effort: minimal, low, medium).
long_description: A Foundry IQ Knowledge Base is the primary configuration unit for Foundry IQ. It references one or more knowledge sources and sets retrieval reasoning effort. Multiple agents in Foundry Agent Service or custom applications (via Azure AI Search APIs) can share the same knowledge base. It integrates with Azure Blob Storage, SharePoint, OneLake, and public web data.

docs_links.title: Create a Foundry IQ knowledge base

label: Foundry IQ Knowledge Source
definition: A connection to indexed or remote content (Blob Storage, SharePoint, OneLake, web) used by a Foundry IQ knowledge base.
long_description: Knowledge sources connect a Foundry IQ knowledge base to data stores. Foundry IQ automates incremental indexing, chunking, and vector embedding generation for each source. ACLs and Microsoft Purview sensitivity labels are synchronized per source so permissions are enforced at query time.

label: Foundry IQ Agentic Retrieval
definition: A multi-query retrieval pipeline that decomposes complex questions into parallel subqueries, semantically reranks results, enforces Purview permissions, and returns grounded answers with citations.
long_description: Foundry IQ Agentic Retrieval uses an LLM from Azure OpenAI (within the Foundry Resource) to plan and decompose user queries into parallel subqueries. It enforces user permissions via Microsoft Purview ACLs and Microsoft Entra identity (caller's identity propagated end-to-end). Results are semantically reranked and returned as extractive answers with source citations. The pipeline supports keyword, vector, and hybrid queries across indexed and remote knowledge sources.

docs_links.title: Agentic retrieval in Azure AI Search

label: Fabric IQ
definition: A semantic intelligence layer for Microsoft Fabric that models business data (ontologies, semantic models, graphs) so agents can reason over OneLake and Power BI analytics.
long_description: Fabric IQ is a standalone IQ workload complementary to Foundry IQ. It exposes business data through ontologies, semantic models, and data agents, enabling agents to query structured analytics in Microsoft Fabric without raw data access.

label: Work IQ
definition: A contextual intelligence layer for Microsoft 365 that captures collaboration signals (documents, meetings, chats, workflows) for agent reasoning.
long_description: Work IQ provides agents insight into how organizations operate by surfacing collaboration context from Microsoft 365. It is complementary to Foundry IQ and Fabric IQ, together forming the three IQ knowledge workloads.

label: Azure OpenAI Deployment
definition: A deployed model instance (GPT, embeddings, vision) within an Azure OpenAI resource.
long_description: Deployments represent specific model versions (e.g., GPT‑4o, GPT‑4 Turbo, text-embedding-3-large) configured with capacity, rate limits, and safety settings. Azure AI Foundry agents and prompt flows call deployments through connections.

label: Azure AI Foundry Agent
definition: A reasoning entity in Azure AI Foundry that uses LLMs, tools, memory, and workflows.
long_description: Agents encapsulate instructions, memory, tools, and model calls. They can collaborate in multi-agent graphs, call Azure OpenAI deployments, query vector stores, and orchestrate workflows. AI‑103 focuses heavily on designing, chaining, and evaluating agents.

docs_links.title: Build agents in Azure AI Foundry

label: Agent Tool
definition: A callable function or API that an agent can invoke to perform external actions.
long_description: Tools allow agents to interact with external systems: Azure Functions, REST APIs, vector search, SQL databases, or custom Python functions. Tools extend agent capabilities beyond pure LLM reasoning.

label: Agent Memory
definition: A persistent memory store used by agents to retain context across interactions.
long_description: Agent memory stores conversation history, facts, or embeddings. It can be backed by vector stores such as Azure AI Search or Cosmos DB. AI‑103 includes configuring memory for multi-turn reasoning.

docs_links.title: Agent memory in Azure AI Foundry

label: Safety Configuration
definition: Safety filters and content moderation settings applied to Azure OpenAI deployments.
long_description: Safety configurations define allowed content categories, thresholds, and moderation behavior. AI‑103 includes configuring safety settings for agents and prompt flows to ensure responsible AI behavior.

label: Prompt Template
definition: A structured prompt used by agents or flows to interact with LLM deployments.
long_description: Prompt templates define system messages, user messages, variables, and formatting. AI‑103 includes designing reusable prompt templates for agents, flows, and RAG pipelines.

docs_links.title: Prompt engineering in Azure AI Foundry

label: Prompt Flow
definition: A workflow of LLM calls, tools, and logic nodes used to build AI applications.
long_description: Prompt Flow is a low-code/no-code orchestration system for chaining prompts, tools, evaluations, and data sources. AI‑103 uses Prompt Flow to build RAG pipelines, multi-step reasoning, and agent workflows.

label: Flow Node
definition: A single step in a Prompt Flow, such as an LLM call, tool invocation, or Python step.
long_description: Flow nodes represent atomic operations in a Prompt Flow. They can call Azure OpenAI deployments, execute Python code, run evaluations, or query vector stores. AI‑103 includes designing and debugging flow nodes.

label: Flow Run
definition: An execution instance of a Prompt Flow with recorded inputs, outputs, and metrics.
long_description: Flow runs capture execution traces, latency, token usage, and intermediate results. AI‑103 includes evaluating flow runs to optimize prompts, tools, and model selection.

label: Evaluation Run
definition: A run that evaluates LLM outputs using metrics, test datasets, or human feedback.
long_description: Evaluation runs measure correctness, relevance, groundedness, safety, and hallucination rates. AI‑103 includes configuring evaluation datasets, metrics, and automated scoring pipelines.

label: Azure AI Search Service
definition: A cloud search service providing full‑text search, semantic search, and vector search capabilities.
long_description: Azure AI Search (formerly Cognitive Search) provides indexing, semantic ranking, hybrid search, and vector search. AI‑103 uses it for Retrieval‑Augmented Generation (RAG), agent memory, and grounding LLM responses. It supports embeddings from Azure OpenAI and integrates with AI Foundry through vector store connections.

label: Search Index
definition: A schema-defined index storing searchable documents, vectors, and metadata.
long_description: Search indexes store structured fields, text content, and vector embeddings. AI‑103 uses indexes to support hybrid search (keyword + vector), semantic ranking, and grounding for LLM applications.

label: Index Schema
definition: A JSON schema defining fields, analyzers, vector dimensions, and scoring profiles.
long_description: Index schemas define the structure of search indexes, including field types, vector embedding dimensions, analyzers, and semantic configurations. AI‑103 includes designing schemas for RAG pipelines.

label: Vector Index
definition: A vector-enabled index storing embeddings for similarity search.
long_description: Vector indexes store high-dimensional embeddings generated by Azure OpenAI or other models. AI‑103 uses vector indexes for semantic retrieval, agent memory, and grounding LLM responses.

docs_links.title: Vector search in Azure AI Search

label: Skillset
definition: A set of cognitive skills applied during indexing to enrich documents.
long_description: Skillsets apply transformations such as OCR, entity extraction, key phrase extraction, and embedding generation. AI‑103 uses skillsets to preprocess documents for RAG pipelines.

label: Indexer
definition: A component that ingests data from external sources into a search index.
long_description: Indexers pull data from Blob Storage, ADLS, SQL, Cosmos DB, or SharePoint. They apply skillsets, generate embeddings, and populate search indexes. AI‑103 uses indexers to automate ingestion for RAG.

label: Data Source
definition: A connection to external data used by indexers to populate search indexes.
long_description: Data sources define the origin of documents for indexing, such as Blob Storage containers, ADLS Gen2 file systems, SQL tables, or Cosmos DB collections. AI‑103 uses data sources to feed RAG pipelines.

docs_links.title: Data sources for Azure AI Search

label: Synonym Map
definition: A mapping of equivalent terms used to improve search recall.
long_description: Synonym maps expand queries with equivalent terms to improve recall. They are used in semantic search and hybrid search scenarios. AI‑103 includes configuring synonym maps for domain-specific terminology.

docs_links.title: Synonym maps in Azure AI Search

label: Vector Store Connection
definition: A connection from Azure AI Foundry to a vector-enabled search or database backend.
long_description: Vector store connections allow agents and prompt flows to query vector indexes for semantic retrieval. Supported backends include Azure AI Search and Cosmos DB vector stores. AI‑103 uses vector stores for grounding and memory.

docs_links.title: Vector store connections in Azure AI Foundry

label: Cosmos DB Vector Store
definition: A Cosmos DB container configured for vector search using high-dimensional embeddings.
long_description: Cosmos DB supports vector search with HNSW indexing. AI‑103 uses Cosmos DB vector stores for agent memory, semantic retrieval, and grounding when low-latency or multi-region replication is required.

docs_links.title: Vector search in Azure Cosmos DB

label: Azure Storage Account
definition: A scalable storage service providing blobs, files, queues, and tables for AI/ML workloads.
long_description: Azure Storage accounts host training data, model artifacts, logs, and intermediate pipeline outputs. AI‑300 uses Blob Storage extensively for data assets, MLflow artifacts, and batch inference inputs/outputs. AI‑103 uses it for document ingestion into Azure AI Search and RAG pipelines.

label: Blob Container
definition: A container within Blob Storage used to store unstructured data such as documents, images, and datasets.
long_description: Blob containers store training datasets, embeddings, documents for indexing, and batch inference inputs. AI‑300 uses blob containers as data assets; AI‑103 uses them as ingestion sources for Azure AI Search and RAG pipelines.

label: ADLS Gen2 Filesystem
definition: A hierarchical filesystem optimized for analytics and large-scale ML workloads.
long_description: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 provides hierarchical namespaces, POSIX permissions, and optimized throughput for ML training data. AI‑300 uses ADLS Gen2 for large datasets, feature store data, and pipeline inputs.

label: Queue Storage
definition: A simple message queue service for decoupled communication.
long_description: Queue Storage is used for lightweight asynchronous workflows. AI‑300 may use queues for triggering batch inference or pipeline steps; AI‑103 may use them for event-driven agent workflows.

label: File Share
definition: A managed SMB file share used for shared storage across compute nodes.
long_description: Azure File Shares are sometimes used for shared datasets or legacy workloads. AI‑300 may use file shares for shared training data or logs across compute instances.

label: Training Dataset
definition: A dataset stored in Blob or ADLS used for ML training jobs.
long_description: Training datasets include CSVs, Parquet files, images, or text corpora. AI‑300 uses datasets as versioned data assets for reproducible training pipelines.

label: Document Corpus
definition: A collection of documents used for indexing and RAG pipelines.
long_description: Document corpora include PDFs, HTML, Markdown, Office files, and text documents. AI‑103 uses document corpora as ingestion sources for Azure AI Search and vector indexing.

docs_links.title: Data ingestion for Azure AI Search

label: Azure SQL Database
definition: A managed relational database service used for structured data storage.
long_description: Azure SQL stores structured data for AI applications, metadata for agents, or tabular datasets for ML. AI‑103 uses SQL as a data source for RAG pipelines; AI‑300 may use it for feature store inputs.

label: SQL Table
definition: A table within Azure SQL used as a structured data source.
long_description: SQL tables store structured records used for indexing, analytics, or ML feature engineering. AI‑103 uses SQL tables as indexer data sources.

label: Cosmos DB Container
definition: A container within Cosmos DB used for JSON documents or vector embeddings.
long_description: Cosmos DB containers store JSON documents, metadata, or vector embeddings. AI‑103 uses Cosmos DB as a vector store backend for agent memory and semantic retrieval.

label: Event Hub
definition: A high-throughput event ingestion service for streaming data.
long_description: Event Hubs ingest telemetry, logs, or real-time data for ML inference or monitoring. AI‑300 may use Event Hubs for real-time model scoring pipelines.

label: Event Stream
definition: A stream of events consumed by ML jobs or real-time inference pipelines.
long_description: Event streams provide real-time data for online inference or monitoring. AI‑300 may integrate event streams into real-time scoring endpoints or drift detection workflows.

label: Azure Data Factory Pipeline
definition: A data integration pipeline used to move, transform, and orchestrate data.
long_description: Data Factory pipelines ingest, transform, and prepare data for ML training or indexing. AI‑300 uses Data Factory for ETL workflows feeding ML pipelines; AI‑103 uses it to prepare documents for RAG ingestion.

label: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
definition: A managed Kubernetes service used to host scalable ML inference workloads.
long_description: AKS provides GPU and CPU node pools for high‑performance real‑time inference. AI‑300 uses AKS for custom model deployments, high‑throughput endpoints, and advanced networking scenarios. It integrates with Azure ML online endpoints for production‑grade serving.

label: AKS Node Pool
definition: A group of virtual machines in an AKS cluster used to run container workloads.
long_description: Node pools define VM sizes, GPU availability, autoscaling, and OS configuration. AI‑300 uses GPU node pools for deep learning inference and CPU pools for lightweight scoring services.

label: AKS Deployment
definition: A Kubernetes deployment hosting ML inference containers.
long_description: AKS deployments run containerized inference servers, often built from Azure ML environments or custom Docker images. AI‑300 uses deployments for scalable, resilient model serving.

label: AKS Service
definition: A Kubernetes service exposing deployed ML inference pods.
long_description: AKS services expose deployments internally or externally. AI‑300 uses load‑balanced services for production inference and integrates them with Azure ML endpoints or API Management.

label: Azure Container Apps
definition: A serverless container platform for hosting microservices and ML inference workloads.
long_description: Container Apps provide autoscaling, Dapr integration, and event‑driven execution. AI‑300 uses Container Apps for lightweight inference endpoints; AI‑103 uses them to host agent tools or custom APIs.

label: Container Apps Environment
definition: A secure environment hosting one or more container apps.
long_description: Container Apps Environments define networking, logging, and scaling boundaries. AI‑300 uses them for grouping inference microservices; AI‑103 uses them for agent tool hosting.

label: Container App
definition: A serverless containerized application running inside a Container Apps Environment.
long_description: Container Apps run inference servers, agent tools, or microservices. They support autoscaling based on HTTP traffic, events, or custom metrics. AI‑103 uses them for tool hosting; AI‑300 uses them for inference.

label: Container App Revision
definition: A versioned snapshot of a Container App configuration.
long_description: Revisions allow blue‑green deployments, traffic splitting, and rollback. AI‑300 uses revisions for safe rollout of inference services.

label: Azure Functions
definition: A serverless compute service for running event-driven code.
long_description: Azure Functions host agent tools, preprocessing logic, or lightweight inference. AI‑103 uses Functions as callable tools for agents; AI‑300 uses them for pipeline triggers or batch orchestration.

label: Function App
definition: A container for hosting one or more Azure Functions.
long_description: Function Apps define runtime settings, identity, networking, and scaling for serverless functions. AI‑103 uses Function Apps to host agent tools that interact with external systems.

label: Azure App Service
definition: A managed platform for hosting web apps and APIs.
long_description: App Service hosts REST APIs, dashboards, and web frontends for AI applications. AI‑103 uses App Service to host agent‑powered apps; AI‑300 uses it to expose ML inference APIs.

label: App Service Deployment Slot
definition: A staging environment for App Service applications.
long_description: Deployment slots allow blue‑green deployments, A/B testing, and safe rollouts. AI‑103 uses slots for agent‑powered apps; AI‑300 uses them for inference API rollouts.

label: Virtual Machine
definition: A general-purpose VM used for custom ML training or hosting inference servers.
long_description: VMs provide full control over OS, drivers, and hardware. AI‑300 uses VMs for custom training environments or GPU workloads not supported by managed compute.

label: Virtual Network (VNet)
definition: A private network boundary for securing Azure resources.
long_description: VNets isolate compute, storage, and AI services. AI‑300 uses VNets for secure ML training and inference; AI‑103 uses VNets for private agent and API hosting.

label: Subnet
definition: A segmented portion of a virtual network used to isolate workloads.
long_description: Subnets host compute resources such as AKS nodes, Container Apps, or VMs. AI‑300 uses subnets to isolate training and inference workloads.

label: Private Endpoint
definition: A private network interface to access Azure services securely.
long_description: Private endpoints allow secure access to Azure ML, Azure OpenAI, Storage, and AI Search without public exposure. AI‑300 and AI‑103 both use private endpoints for compliance‑sensitive workloads.

label: Network Security Group (NSG)
definition: A firewall-like resource controlling inbound and outbound traffic.
long_description: NSGs enforce traffic rules for subnets and network interfaces using allow/deny rules based on source, destination, port, and protocol. They are a core building block for securing VNets, AKS nodes, Container Apps, and VMs. In AI-300 and AI-103 scenarios, NSGs help restrict access to training clusters, inference endpoints, and data stores to only approved networks and services.

label: RAG Pipeline
definition: A pipeline that enriches model responses by retrieving relevant external knowledge.
long_description: A RAG pipeline combines embedding generation, vector retrieval, reranking, and LLM prompting to produce grounded, factual responses. In Azure AI Foundry, RAG pipelines integrate Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB vector stores, and Azure OpenAI models to build enterprise-grade generative AI applications.

label: Retriever
definition: A component that retrieves relevant documents or vectors for grounding model responses.
long_description: Retrievers query vector stores or hybrid search systems to fetch semantically similar content. Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB, and pgvector-enabled PostgreSQL are commonly used retrievers in enterprise RAG architectures.

docs_links.title: Vector search in Azure AI Search

label: Embedding Model
definition: A model that converts text or images into numerical vector representations.
long_description: Embedding models such as text-embedding-3-large or multimodal embedding models generate dense vectors used for semantic search, clustering, and retrieval. In Azure AI Foundry, embedding models are used to index documents for RAG pipelines.

label: Embedding Vector
definition: A high-dimensional numeric vector representing semantic meaning.
long_description: Embedding vectors encode semantic similarity, enabling vector search and retrieval. They are stored in vector databases such as Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB, or pgvector-enabled PostgreSQL.

docs_links.title: High-dimensional vector embeddings in Azure Cosmos DB

label: Chunking Strategy
definition: A method for splitting documents into semantically meaningful segments.
long_description: Chunking strategies determine how text is divided before embedding. Common approaches include fixed-size chunks, semantic chunking, and recursive text splitting. Proper chunking improves retrieval accuracy and reduces hallucinations.

docs_links.title: Developing a RAG solution – Chunking phase

label: Reranker
definition: A model that reorders retrieved documents to improve relevance.
long_description: Rerankers evaluate retrieved documents using cross-encoder models or LLM scoring. Azure AI Search supports integrated reranking using semantic ranking and hybrid scoring.

docs_links.title: Semantic ranking in Azure AI Search

label: Hybrid Search
definition: A retrieval method combining vector search with keyword search.
long_description: Hybrid search blends lexical retrieval (BM25) with vector similarity to improve accuracy on both semantic and keyword-heavy queries. Azure AI Search supports hybrid scoring natively.

docs_links.title: Hybrid search in Azure AI Search

label: Vector Store
definition: A database optimized for storing and querying embedding vectors.
long_description: Vector stores enable fast similarity search for RAG applications. Azure supports multiple vector backends including Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and third-party systems like Pinecone and Milvus.

docs_links.title: Vector store connectors in Semantic Kernel

label: Azure AI Search Vector Retrieval
definition: A vector-enabled search engine supporting hybrid and semantic retrieval.
long_description: Azure AI Search provides vector indexing, hybrid search, semantic ranking, and integrated reranking. It is the primary vector store for enterprise RAG workloads in Azure AI Foundry.

docs_links.title: Vector search in Azure AI Search

label: Cosmos DB Vector Store
definition: A globally distributed NoSQL database with integrated vector search.
long_description: Azure Cosmos DB supports vector indexing and similarity search, enabling low-latency RAG applications with global distribution and multi-region writes.

label: PostgreSQL pgvector
definition: A PostgreSQL extension enabling vector similarity search.
long_description: Azure Database for PostgreSQL supports pgvector for storing and querying embeddings. It is commonly used for custom RAG architectures requiring SQL joins and relational modeling.

label: Redis Vector Store
definition: An in-memory vector database built on Redis modules.
long_description: Redis supports vector similarity search using RediSearch. It is used for ultra-low-latency RAG workloads and caching retrieved vectors.

docs_links.title: Redis vector store connector in Semantic Kernel

label: Milvus Vector Store
definition: An open-source vector database optimized for large-scale embeddings.
long_description: Milvus provides distributed vector indexing and high-throughput similarity search. It is used for large RAG workloads requiring billions of vectors.

docs_links.title: Milvus vector store connector in Semantic Kernel

label: Pinecone Vector Store
definition: A managed vector database for enterprise RAG workloads.
long_description: Pinecone provides fully managed vector indexing, filtering, and hybrid search. It integrates with Azure AI Foundry through custom connections and agent tools.

docs_links.title: Pinecone vector store connector in Semantic Kernel

label: ChromaDB Vector Store
