definition: A lightweight open-source vector store for local or embedded RAG workloads.
long_description: ChromaDB is commonly used for prototyping RAG pipelines, agent memory, and local development. It supports persistent and in-memory vector indexing.

docs_links.title: Chroma vector store connector in Semantic Kernel

label: Text Embedding Model
definition: A model that converts text into dense numerical vectors for semantic search and retrieval.
long_description: Text embedding models such as text-embedding-3-large generate high-dimensional vectors that capture semantic meaning. These vectors are used in RAG pipelines, clustering, similarity search, and document retrieval. Azure AI Foundry integrates embedding models with vector stores like Azure AI Search and Cosmos DB.

label: Image Embedding Model
definition: A model that converts images into vector representations for similarity search and multimodal retrieval.
long_description: Image embedding models encode visual content into dense vectors. These embeddings support multimodal RAG, image similarity search, and hybrid text-image retrieval. Azure AI Foundry supports image embeddings through multimodal models like Florence and GPT-4o.

docs_links.title: Generate image embeddings with Azure AI Foundry

label: GPT-4o
definition: A multimodal model capable of processing text, images, audio, and video.
long_description: GPT-4o is a state-of-the-art multimodal model that supports text generation, vision understanding, audio transcription, and multimodal reasoning. In Azure AI Foundry, GPT-4o powers advanced agent workflows, multimodal RAG, and enterprise generative AI applications.

label: Florence Vision Model
definition: A foundation model for image understanding, captioning, tagging, and OCR.
long_description: Florence is Microsoft's multimodal vision model used for image captioning, object detection, OCR, and visual embeddings. It integrates with Azure AI Vision and Azure AI Foundry for multimodal applications and RAG pipelines involving images.

label: Phi-3 Vision
definition: A lightweight multimodal model supporting text and image understanding.
long_description: Phi-3 Vision is a compact multimodal model optimized for cost-efficient inference. It supports image understanding, captioning, and multimodal reasoning. Azure AI Foundry uses Phi-3 Vision for agent tools, RAG enrichment, and lightweight multimodal applications.

label: Whisper Speech-to-Text
definition: A model that converts spoken audio into text.
long_description: Whisper is a multilingual speech recognition model capable of transcribing audio with high accuracy. Azure AI Foundry integrates Whisper for agent tools, multimodal pipelines, and audio-based RAG workflows.

label: Azure Speech Text-to-Speech
definition: A model that converts text into natural-sounding speech.
long_description: Azure Speech provides neural text-to-speech capabilities for generating human-like audio. It is used in conversational agents, accessibility applications, and multimodal AI experiences.

label: Phi-3 Code Model
definition: A lightweight model optimized for code generation, completion, and reasoning.
long_description: Phi-3 Code is a compact model designed for code synthesis, debugging, and reasoning tasks. It powers agent tools, developer copilots, and automation workflows in Azure AI Foundry.

label: OCR Model
definition: A model that extracts text from images and scanned documents.
long_description: OCR models convert visual text into machine-readable text. Azure Document Intelligence and Florence Vision provide OCR capabilities for document processing, multimodal RAG, and agent workflows.

label: Natural Language Understanding Model
definition: A model that extracts meaning, intent, and structure from text.
long_description: NLU models perform tasks such as entity extraction, intent classification, sentiment analysis, and semantic parsing. Azure AI Language provides NLU capabilities for enterprise applications and agent reasoning.

label: Summarization Model
definition: A model that condenses long text into concise summaries.
long_description: Summarization models generate extractive or abstractive summaries. Azure AI Foundry uses summarization for document processing, RAG enrichment, and agent reasoning.

label: Translation Model
definition: A model that translates text between languages.
long_description: Translation models support multilingual applications, cross-language RAG, and global agent deployments. Azure AI Translator provides neural translation capabilities.

label: Sentiment Analysis Model
definition: A model that classifies sentiment in text.
long_description: Sentiment analysis models detect positive, negative, or neutral sentiment. They are used in customer analytics, agent reasoning, and content moderation workflows.

docs_links.title: Sentiment analysis in Azure AI Language

label: Named Entity Recognition Model
definition: A model that identifies entities such as people, locations, and organizations in text.
long_description: NER models extract structured information from unstructured text. They support document processing, RAG enrichment, and agent reasoning in Azure AI Foundry.

docs_links.title: Named Entity Recognition (NER) in Azure AI Language

label: Key Phrase Extraction Model
definition: A model that extracts important phrases from text.
long_description: Key phrase extraction models identify the most relevant concepts in a document. They are used for metadata generation, RAG preprocessing, and document indexing.

docs_links.title: Key phrase extraction in Azure AI Language

label: Prompt Evaluation
definition: A process for assessing prompt quality, clarity, and effectiveness.
long_description: Prompt evaluation measures how well a prompt elicits accurate, safe, and consistent model responses. Azure AI Foundry supports automated prompt evaluation using metrics such as groundedness, relevance, coherence, and safety. It is used to refine system, user, and assistant prompts in agent workflows.

docs_links.title: Evaluate prompts in Azure AI Foundry

label: Model Evaluation
definition: A process for measuring model performance using quantitative and qualitative metrics.
long_description: Model evaluation assesses accuracy, groundedness, toxicity, bias, and robustness. Azure AI Foundry provides evaluation pipelines for LLMs, embeddings, and multimodal models. Evaluations can be automated using Prompt Flow or integrated with human review.

label: Safety Evaluation
definition: An evaluation process that measures whether model outputs comply with safety and ethical guidelines.
long_description: Safety evaluation detects harmful, toxic, biased, or policy-violating outputs. Azure AI Foundry includes automated safety scoring, red-teaming tests, and integration with Azure AI Content Safety. Safety evaluation is required for production-grade generative AI applications.

label: Red Teaming Evaluation
definition: A structured process for stress-testing AI systems using adversarial prompts.
long_description: Red teaming evaluation identifies vulnerabilities by intentionally probing models with harmful, adversarial, or policy-violating prompts. Azure AI Foundry supports automated red-teaming workflows and integrates with PyRIT for advanced adversarial testing.

label: Content Safety Filters
definition: Automated filters that detect and block harmful or unsafe content.
long_description: Content safety filters classify and block harmful outputs such as hate speech, violence, sexual content, and self-harm. Azure AI Content Safety provides configurable filters and severity scoring for text and images, enabling safe deployment of generative AI systems.

label: AI Red Teaming Agent
definition: An autonomous agent designed to probe AI systems for vulnerabilities.
long_description: AI red teaming agents generate adversarial prompts, explore edge cases, and identify unsafe model behaviors. They are used to validate safety mitigations and ensure compliance with responsible AI standards. Azure AI Foundry supports custom red-teaming agents built with Prompt Flow and agent tools.

label: PyRIT
definition: Microsoft's open-source AI red-teaming framework for evaluating generative AI systems.
long_description: PyRIT (Python Risk Identification Toolkit) automates adversarial testing of AI systems. It generates harmful prompts, evaluates model responses, and identifies safety vulnerabilities. PyRIT integrates with Azure AI Foundry for enterprise red-teaming workflows.

label: Content Moderation
definition: A process for detecting and managing harmful or policy-violating content.
long_description: Content moderation uses automated classifiers and human review to detect harmful content. Azure AI Content Safety provides moderation APIs for text and images, enabling safe deployment of chatbots, agents, and generative AI applications.

label: Safety Evaluation Dataset
definition: A curated dataset used to evaluate model safety and detect harmful behavior.
long_description: Safety evaluation datasets contain adversarial prompts, harmful content examples, and edge-case scenarios. They are used to benchmark model robustness and validate safety mitigations. Azure AI Foundry supports custom and built-in safety datasets.

docs_links.title: Safety evaluation datasets in MLflow (Azure Databricks)

label: Safety Configuration
definition: A configuration defining safety rules, thresholds, and filters for AI systems.
long_description: Safety configurations specify allowed content, severity thresholds, and filtering rules for generative AI systems. Azure AI Foundry uses safety configurations to enforce content safety policies across agents, prompts, and endpoints.

docs_links.title: Safety configuration in Azure AI Content Safety

label: System Prompt
definition: A high-level instruction that defines the AI's behavior, rules, and persona.
long_description: The system prompt establishes the model's role, constraints, and behavioral guidelines. It provides global context for all subsequent interactions. In Azure AI Foundry, system prompts are used to configure agents, flows, and evaluation scenarios.

label: User Prompt
definition: A prompt containing the user's request, question, or instruction.
long_description: User prompts represent the direct input from the end user. They drive the model's task execution and are combined with system and assistant prompts to form the full conversational context. In Azure AI Foundry, user prompts are evaluated for clarity, safety, and intent.

label: Assistant Prompt
definition: A model-generated response that continues the conversation or performs a task.
long_description: Assistant prompts are generated by the model based on system and user prompts. They may include reasoning, tool calls, or structured outputs. In Azure AI Foundry, assistant prompts are evaluated for groundedness, safety, and task completion.

label: Prompt Flow Data Connection
definition: A connection that links Prompt Flow to external data sources.
long_description: Prompt Flow data connections provide access to datasets, vector stores, storage accounts, and APIs. They enable evaluation, batch testing, and RAG workflows. Azure AI Foundry supports connections to Azure Storage, AI Search, Cosmos DB, and custom endpoints.

docs_links.title: Prompt flow data connections in Azure Machine Learning

label: Prompt Flow Evaluation Component
definition: A component that evaluates model outputs using metrics or LLM-based scoring.
long_description: Evaluation components in Prompt Flow compute metrics such as groundedness, relevance, fluency, and safety. They support automated testing of prompts, agents, and RAG pipelines. Azure AI Foundry includes built-in evaluators and supports custom scoring logic.

label: Agent Memory (Vector Memory)
definition: A memory system that stores embeddings for long-term recall.
long_description: Vector memory enables agents to recall past interactions, documents, or facts using semantic search. It is implemented using vector stores such as Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB, or pgvector. Azure AI Foundry agents use vector memory for contextual reasoning and personalization.

label: Agent Memory (Episodic Memory)
definition: A memory system that stores short-term conversational context.
long_description: Episodic memory captures recent interactions, enabling agents to maintain continuity across turns. It is typically stored in session state or short-term buffers. Azure AI Foundry agents use episodic memory to support multi-turn reasoning.

docs_links.title: Episodic memory (conversation history) in Agent Framework

label: Agent Memory (Tool Memory)
definition: A memory system that stores results from tool calls for reuse.
long_description: Tool memory captures outputs from API calls, database queries, or external tools. It allows agents to reuse information without repeating expensive operations. Azure AI Foundry agents use tool memory to optimize workflows and reduce latency.

docs_links.title: Tool memory (tool call history) in Agent Framework

label: Agent Planning
definition: A process where an agent decomposes a task into steps or tool calls.
long_description: Agent planning enables multi-step reasoning, task decomposition, and decision-making. Models like GPT-4o generate plans that orchestrate tools, memory, and prompts. Azure AI Foundry supports planning through agent graphs and Prompt Flow.

docs_links.title: Agent planning in Agent Framework workflows

label: Tool Calling
definition: A mechanism that allows models to invoke external tools or APIs.
long_description: Tool calling enables agents to interact with external systems such as databases, APIs, or custom logic. Azure AI Foundry supports structured tool calling using JSON schemas, enabling deterministic and safe execution of external actions.

label: Function Calling
definition: A structured interface that allows models to call predefined functions.
long_description: Function calling provides a schema-based mechanism for models to request execution of specific functions. It is used for deterministic tool invocation, data retrieval, and workflow automation. Azure AI Foundry supports function calling for agents and Prompt Flow nodes.

label: Agent Tool
definition: An external capability that an agent can invoke to perform actions or retrieve data.
long_description: Agent tools extend the capabilities of LLMs by enabling them to call APIs, run code, query databases, or interact with enterprise systems. Azure AI Foundry supports tool registration, authentication, and schema-based invocation.

label: Agent Graph
definition: A structured graph defining how agents, tools, and prompts interact.
long_description: Agent graphs define the flow of information between prompts, tools, memory, and planning components. They enable complex multi-agent workflows in Azure AI Foundry, supporting orchestration, branching, and conditional logic.

docs_links.title: Agent Graph in Agent Framework workflows

label: Agent Role
definition: A defined persona or function assigned to an agent within a workflow.
long_description: Agent roles define the purpose and behavior of an agent, such as researcher, planner, critic, or executor. Roles influence system prompts and guide how agents collaborate in multi-agent systems.

label: Agent Execution Engine
definition: The runtime system that executes agent workflows, tool calls, and planning steps.
long_description: The agent execution engine orchestrates prompts, memory, tools, and planning logic. Azure AI Foundry provides a scalable execution engine for running multi-agent workflows, integrating with Prompt Flow, vector stores, and external APIs.

docs_links.title: Agent Execution Engine in Agent Framework

label: Fine-Tuning Job
definition: A job that trains a base model on custom data to specialize its behavior.
long_description: Fine-tuning jobs adapt foundation models to domain-specific tasks using supervised datasets. Azure AI Foundry supports fine-tuning for models such as GPT, Phi, and embedding models. Fine-tuning improves accuracy, consistency, and task alignment for enterprise applications.

label: LoRA Adapter
definition: A lightweight adapter that enables efficient fine-tuning of large models.
long_description: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) injects trainable matrices into a model’s layers, enabling fine-tuning with minimal compute and storage. Azure AI Foundry supports LoRA-based fine-tuning for cost-efficient customization of LLMs and multimodal models.

docs_links.title: LoRA adapters for fine-tuning in Azure Machine Learning

label: Fine-Tuned Model
definition: A model that has been adapted to a specific task using custom training data.
long_description: Fine-tuned models incorporate domain-specific knowledge and improved task performance. Azure AI Foundry registers fine-tuned models as new versions, enabling deployment to endpoints, agents, and RAG pipelines.

docs_links.title: Fine-tuned models in Azure Machine Learning

label: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
definition: A training method that aligns models using human preference data.
long_description: DPO optimizes models directly on preference pairs without requiring reinforcement learning. It improves helpfulness, safety, and alignment with human expectations. Azure AI Foundry supports DPO workflows for aligning LLMs with enterprise policies.

docs_links.title: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) in Azure AI

label: Reward Model
definition: A model that scores outputs based on human preferences or safety criteria.
long_description: Reward models evaluate responses for helpfulness, correctness, and safety. They are used in DPO, RLHF, and alignment pipelines. Azure AI Foundry supports training reward models using labeled preference datasets.

docs_links.title: Reward modeling in RLHF (Azure Machine Learning)

label: Preference Dataset
definition: A dataset containing human-labeled preference pairs for alignment training.
long_description: Preference datasets include pairs of model outputs ranked by quality or safety. They are used to train reward models and DPO pipelines. Azure AI Foundry supports importing custom preference datasets for enterprise alignment workflows.

label: Alignment Pipeline
definition: A workflow that aligns models using preference data, reward models, and safety constraints.
long_description: Alignment pipelines orchestrate DPO, RLHF, safety evaluation, and fine-tuning steps. Azure AI Foundry supports alignment pipelines through Prompt Flow, custom training jobs, and integrated safety evaluations.

docs_links.title: Alignment pipelines (RLHF/DPO) in Azure Machine Learning

label: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)
definition: A training method that uses reward models and reinforcement learning to align model behavior.
long_description: RLHF trains models using human feedback and reward signals to improve helpfulness and safety. While more complex than DPO, RLHF provides strong alignment for conversational agents. Azure AI Foundry supports RLHF through custom training pipelines.

label: Safety-Aligned Model
definition: A model trained to follow safety rules, avoid harmful content, and comply with policies.
long_description: Safety-aligned models incorporate safety constraints, preference optimization, and red-teaming feedback. Azure AI Foundry uses safety-aligned models for production agents, RAG systems, and enterprise applications requiring strict compliance.

label: Alignment Evaluation
definition: A process that verifies whether a model meets alignment and safety requirements.
long_description: Alignment evaluation measures whether a model follows instructions, avoids harmful content, and adheres to enterprise policies. Azure AI Foundry integrates alignment evaluation with safety scoring, red-teaming, and automated prompt testing.

docs_links.title: Alignment evaluation for safe AI models

label: Training Dataset
definition: A labeled dataset used to fine-tune or align a model.
long_description: Training datasets contain examples of desired model behavior. They may include instructions, demonstrations, preference pairs, or safety annotations. Azure AI Foundry supports dataset versioning, validation, and secure storage.

docs_links.title: Training datasets in Azure Machine Learning

label: Evaluation Dataset
definition: A dataset used to measure model performance, safety, and alignment.
long_description: Evaluation datasets include test cases for accuracy, groundedness, safety, and robustness. Azure AI Foundry supports automated evaluation pipelines using Prompt Flow and LLM-based scoring.

docs_links.title: Evaluation datasets in Azure Machine Learning

label: Alignment Configuration
definition: A configuration defining alignment objectives, constraints, and training parameters.
long_description: Alignment configurations specify reward functions, preference weights, safety constraints, and optimization settings. Azure AI Foundry uses alignment configurations to orchestrate DPO, RLHF, and safety-aligned training.

label: Safety Constraint
definition: A rule or restriction applied during training to prevent harmful behavior.
long_description: Safety constraints enforce content rules, policy boundaries, and behavioral limits during training. They are used in alignment pipelines to ensure models avoid harmful or non-compliant outputs.

label: Alignment Report
definition: A report summarizing alignment results, safety scores, and compliance status.
long_description: Alignment reports document evaluation metrics, safety findings, and compliance outcomes. Azure AI Foundry generates alignment reports to support audits, governance, and responsible AI reviews.
